TLleuTJIMtU.title: Emmet Cohen - Well You Needn't (Official Audio)
TLleuTJIMtU.description:
Emmet Cohen  ——
Official audio for “Well You Needn't” by Emmet Cohen, from the album Universal Truth on Mack Avenue Records.
 
Preorder/Presave Universal Truth: https://lnk.to/emmetcohenYT 

Universal Truth: The Playlist https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLq0CRabXDXSxUxVJNx_DQ15CCa-QDC99U

Emmet Cohen, DownBeat Magazine’s 2025 Pianist of the Year and critically acclaimed composer and bandleader, has been busy. Busy touring a Miles Davis & John Coltrane tribute to sold-out audiences across the world, busy running Live from Emmet’s Place as the “most highly watched regular online jazz show in the world,” and busy recording his new album, Universal Truth. With Joe Farnsworth, Yasushi Nakamura, Jeremy Pelt, Tivon Pennicott and guests Ron Carter and George Coleman, Emmet brings his celebrated tribute to the studio, pairing timeless classics (“Well You Needn’t,” “My Funny Valentine”) with new originals that honor jazz’s royalty while boldly carrying their tradition forward.

PERSONNEL
Emmet Cohen: Piano
Joe Farnsworth: Drums
Ron Carter: Bass
Jeremy Pelt: Trumpet

0:00 Hallucinations (Bud Powell) / Budo (Miles Davis) 
7:40 Emmet Cohen Introduces The Trio: Kyle Poole, drums; Philip Norris, bass 🥁🎻🎹
8:40 Introduction of Vocalist, Georgia Heers //
Georgia’s Return to Emmet’s Place following her Broadway run on “Good Night & Good Luck” ✨
9:40 I Love Paris (Cole Porter) 🇫🇷💋
17:10 Georgia Heers Greets the Living Room & Livestream Audience // Reflection on Past Appearances & the soul-lifting moment honoring our Funmi Ononaiye
19:12 A Sleepin’ Bee 🍯 (Harold Arlen; lyr. Truman Capote, H. Arlen) - ft. Georgia Heers, vocals & Phil Norris, bass / 21:46 Trio Break 
27:50 Getting Caught Up In The Swing 🎼🎶 // 🎭 Recounting Georgia’s first Broadway Appearance at the Winter Garden Theatre & CNN Livestream of Stage Production 🎵📡🖥️
29:44 My Shining Hour ✨ (Harold Arlen) - ft. Georgia Heers, vocals 🌌
36:25 Picking Up The Speed 🔥 // 36:54 The Inherent Joy of Playing This Music / the Portrayal of  ‘Ella’ a 1950’s Vocalist; the Art of ‘Singing the Melody’ In Theater🎙️// 38:04 Georgia Recaps A Profound Moment from “Good Night & Good Luck” — Special Rendition of How High The Moon 🙏🏼
39:18 How High The Moon 🌕 (Morgan Lewis; Nancy Hamilton) - ft. Georgia Heers, vocals
46:49 A Thankful Spirit for the Range of Support within this Music Community // 5th to Last Performance at the Apartment & Savoring Every Note // Appreciation of Presenting Sponsors 
49:24 Special Recognition of Sound Engineer Kelvin Grant 🎤🔊👑🏆// His Unbroken Dedication & Expertise as Emmet’s Place Mastermind of Sound // (Ongoing involvements with Samara Joy & Cecile McLorin Salvant)
50:11 Heartfelt Appreciation to the TGIM Facebook Group for Their Evening Sponsorship 💟 🙌🏽♾️
51:17 Introduction of a Betty Carter Composition
51:53 Droppin’ Things (comp. Betty Carter) - ft. Georgia Heers, vocals 💧
1:00:11 I Only Have Eyes For You 💎 (Harry Warren; lyr. Al Dubin) // 1:03:24 Trio Break / EC Piano solo 
1:11:49 All Of Me 🎂 (Gerald Marks; Seymour Simons) - ft. Georgia Heers, vocals & Benny Benack, III, trumpet //  1:13:48 Benny Benack trumpet solo 🎺 / 1:15:35 EC stride piano solo 🎹
1:21:22 Introduction of A Beautiful Ballad, & Connecting with Castmembers // (A Clooney Favorite 🎼)
1:22:57 Tenderly (Walter Gross; Jack Lawrence) 🫂✨
1:29:56 Introduction of Brandon Goldberg - fellow Miami pianist, known since early childhood 
1:30:47 Blues Piano Introduction by Brandon 🔹
1:31:37 (In the Evening) When The Sun Goes Down (Leroy Carr) - ft. Georgia Heers, vocals 💙 // 1:32:54 Piano solo, B. Goldberg  // 1:34:54 Trumpet solos: B. Benack III & Max Boico 🎺 // 1:37:18 4-Hands at Piano // 1:41:51 William Hill III joins at the Keys — A Soulful Overflow of Goodwill & Good Vibes At the Crib 🌃🤟🏽🖤🎶
1:43:11 Georgia Resumes Blues // 1:43:59 joined by vocalist Christian Wiig // 1:44:50 4-Hands at Piano with EC and WHIII 🔥🎵
1:46:12 Emmet Closes It Out With Musicians —
(EC3, BBIII, WHIII, & Company) // Words of Appreciation to Georgia Heers 💜🎙️ Musicians, & Evening Sponsors  🎹🥁🎻🎺

IJmsd99g_y0.title: Emmet Cohen Trio - Live in Thailand (2023)
IJmsd99g_y0.description:
Recorded Live at Thailand International Jazz Conference - January, 2023

Emmet Cohen - Piano
Philip Norris - Bass
Kyle Poole - Drums

Thailand International Jazz Conference (TIJC) is a jazz music event initiated in 2009 by Mahidol University College of Music, aimed at presenting top quality jazz music to the public and also offering jazz education to the interested musicians (both students and general publics). To achieve our aim, TIJC is held in the university venue and consists of three main elements, i.e., music performance, Jazz Education Activities (Discussion), and Jazz Composition Competition, which are opened both for general professional jazz musicians. In addition to the existing elements, an educational event "TIJC Youth Camp" was initiated in 2020, offering all participants great chances to meet, perform with, and learn from the professional jazz musicians.

2xxUILwaO5k.description:
Live From Emmet's Place will be celebrating its 100th episode tomorrow night with special guests Sean Jones and Tivon Pennicott! We hope you will join us for this momentous occaision! In the meantime, here is a compilation of some of our favorite solos from the past two years.

0:00 This Time The Dream’s On Me  🪄🎼🎶🌙 (Harold Arlen; lyr. Johnny Mercer) - ft. Steve Nelson, vibes / 5:35 Emmet Cohen piano solo / 8:40 Peter Washington bass solo / 11:01  some dream-like trading ✨/ 13:56 Steve wraps up the dream sequence 
15:52 Up Jumped Spring 🍃(comp. Freddie Hubbard) - ft. Steve Nelson vibraphone Introduction / 18:12 EC piano solo / 22:12 Steve Nelson vibes solo / 25:20 Joe Farnsworth drum solo 🔥
*~*~*~*~*~*~*~*~*~*~*~
28:43 God Bless The Child 💎🙏🏼🙌🏾 (Billie Holliday, Arthur Herzog, Jr.) - ft. Steve Nelson vibraphone Introduction / 35:07 Emmet Cohen piano solo / 37:26 Steve blesses us on the bridge & takes us home ⛲️
40:15 Emmet Cohen Welcomes the Audience:
A heartwarming gathering & notes of nostalgia…our 2nd to the last performance in the Harlem apartment… our roaring 20’s rent party of the 21st century draws near to new digs & a new era 🎉🎶📖🌎
*~*~*~*~*~*~*~*~*~*~*~
40:51 Emmet Introduces the Musicians: Joe Farnsworth, drums - celebrating a successful release of his latest album, “The Big Room” 🥁 Peter Washington, bass 🎻- a legend of the NY scene, bearing generations of elite swing & scholarship on his upright; Steve Nelson, vibraphone - our featured guest & vibraphone royalty; a creative brilliance, sharpened by tradition & fashioned for the future  🔥🔭
44:36 Rising Trumpeter Mejedi Owusu is Welcomed to the Living Room stage  🎺
*~*~*~*~*~*~*~*~*~*~*~
46:03 Voyage Kenny Barron 🛳️- ft. Mejedi Owusu, trumpet / 49:41 Steve Nelson vibraphone solo 🔹/ 51:43 Emmet Cohen piano solo 🎵 / 55:17 a quick trading odyssey / 55:50 Joe Farnsworth drum solo 💫 / 57:36 Mejedi concludes the voyage 🎯
58:45 Steve Nelson Expresses Appreciation for his Emmet’s Place appearance & honors his fellow musicians as the measure of true greatness 🏆🔥💯 
59:47 Bluesology (Milt Jackson) - ft. Steve Nelson, vibraphone 🎼💙 / 1:03:04 Mejedi Owusu trumpet solo / 1:05:06 Emmet Cohen piano solo / 1:08:32 Peter Washington bass solo / 1:11:11 Steve resumes Milt’s tune with his sophisticated swing 🍸
*~*~*~*~*~*~*~*~*~*~*~
1:13:29 Never Let Me Go 🙌🏽🪁 (Jay Livingston; lyr. Raymond B. Evans) - ft. Steve Nelson vibraphone / 1:17:44 Emmet Cohen piano solo / 1:19:49 Peter Washington bass solo 
1:23:55 Gratitude to Evening Sponsors 🌎♾️/ 1:25:52  Anticipation of what’s to come…& a subtle melancholy of saying farewell to our beloved 5 floors in Harlem 🎶 🔭🤍🙏🏽
*~*~*~*~*~*~*~*~*~*~*~
1:26:15 Softly, As In A Morning Sunrise  🪶(Sigmund Romberg; lyr. Oscar Hammerstein, II) - ft. Steve Nelson, vibraphone intro 🪄 / 1:27:34 Mejedi Owusu trumpet solo / 1:30:09 Steve Nelson vibes solo 🔹/ 1:32:44 Emmet Cohen piano solo / 1:36:50 Steve & EC brilliantly ‘talk it out‘🔥🎶 🎹 /  1:38:41 Mejedi & Joe exchange blazing notes / 1:39:38 Joe Farnsworth drum solo 🔥🔥/  1:42:20 Cats return to the sunrise…& rise to the occasion 🌄👏🏽 
*~*~*~*~*~*~*~*~*~*~*~
1:43:49 Mejedi Owusu Shares Deep Respect for his musical journey from England to the Harlem living room stage 🇬🇧/ Admiration for the great musicians surrounding him 🗽🌃🍎
1:44:10 Emmet’s Place Welcomes Owusu’s fellow Julliard Cats: Terry Washington, trumpet, & Gustavo Cruz, tenor 📚🎷🎺
1:45:43 Anthropology (comp. Charlie Parker; Dizzy Gillespie) 🌍🤎 - ft. Peter Washington bass intro /  1:47:11 Cats kick-off a Parker classic / 1:46:39 Terry Washington trumpet solo / 1:48:38 Gustavo Cruz tenor solo / 1:50:05 Mejedi Owusu trumpet solo / 1:52:06 Steve Nelson vibraphone solo 🙌🏽 / 1:54:27 Emmet Cohen piano solo 🔥 / 1:56:39 Peter Washington bass solo 🏆/ 
1:58:24 An Anthropology case study: the impact of swing on humanity in a crowded Harlem crib 🎶🔥🏡
2:00:42 Emmet Closes the Evening with a Warm Send-off & Heartfelt Recognition of all Musicians ♥️

Video Premiere for Alex Weitz's new album "Rule of Thirds" out April 28th, 2023 

Streaming Links: https://found.ee/ruleofthirds
Bandcamp Link (Physical/Digital Purchases): https://alexweitzgroup.bandcamp.com/album/rule-of-thirds

Alex Weitz - saxophone, composer (https://www.youtube.com/alexweitzjazz)
Emmet Cohen - piano
Tal Cohen - piano
Yotam Silberstein - guitar
Marcus Printup - trumpet 
Ben Tiberio - bass
Ari Hoenig - drums 
Michael Piolet - drums 

Track List:
1) The Hive (featuring Emmet Cohen and Ari Hoenig)
2) Nocturne in C Sharp Minor (featuring Yotam Silberstein)
3) Rude Awakening
4) Harlem Lullaby (featuring Ben Tiberio and Tal Cohen)
5) Odyssey
6) Convent Court (featuring Emmet Cohen and Marcus Printup)
7) Rule of Thirds
8) Love for Sale (featuring Ari Hoenig and Tal Cohen)
9) Sonata for Fred (Duo with Emmet Cohen)

special thanks to:

RW1JtfKURvk.title: Sullivan Fortner "Alfie" Transcription!
RW1JtfKURvk.description:
Transcription by Fred Greene - www.patreon.com/fredgreene / @Why_Jazz 

I was hangin with my man Sullivan Fortner who played this beautiful impromptu version of Burt Bacharach’s “Alfie.” Fred Green thought it was stunning too and transcribed it... we’d share it with you!

AmIZY51DKmA.title: Gary, Indiana | Benny Benack III &  Emmet Cohen
AmIZY51DKmA.description:
Benny Benack III: Trumpet/Vocals
Emmet Cohen: Piano

Follow Benny Benack III for more content:
➤ Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/bennybenackjazz
➤ Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/bbjazziii
➤ TikTok: http://www.tiktok.com/@bbjazziii
➤ Website: http://www.bennybenackjazz.com
➤ Subscribe: @bbjazzIII  


For Benny Benack III, Gary, Indiana is the throwback of all throwbacks. “Gary, Indiana is the first song I ever performed in my life as a professional,” he shares. “It was me, my mom played piano with me, and I would be like 8 years old, walking out onstage in the middle of my dad’s concert. My mom would hold my hand, and we’d go out and I’d sing Gary, Indiana.”

Benack’s parents couldn’t make the gig this time around, so instead he clears the floor for and makes it a rollicking duet with pianist Emmet Cohen. “I also wanted to feature Emmet’s stride playing,” Benack notes. “I think he’s one of the great masters of that style.”

“Gary, Indiana” is now streaming on all platforms via La Reserve Records and Bandstand Presents.

Video filming by: Alex Weitz
Editing by: Bandstand Presents

9:02 Stairway To The Stars (Matty Malneck & Frank Signorelli; lyr. Mitchell Parish) 🌌
15:37 An Elling Prelude & Soulful Calibration 🧘🏻
17:11 A New Body and Soul 🫂🌎✨ (comp. Johnny Green; lyric interpretation by Kurt Elling ) / 23:48 Emmet Cohen piano solo / 25:49 Philip Norris bass solo 🔥/ 28:00 Bryan Carter drum solo 
31:32 Emmet Introduces Tenor Saxophonist Jacob Chung to the Living Room Stage 🎼🎷
32:00 Long As You’re Living (Julian Priester, Tommy Turrentine; lyr. Oscar Brown Jr.; Abbey Lincoln & Max Roach)  🧬🎶 - ft. Kurt Elling, vocals and Jacob Chung, tenor sax / 34:37 Jacob Chung sax solo / 36:10 Emmet Cohen piano solo 🔥/ 38:25 Kurt Elling scat solo (‘a primal cosmic remembering’) / 40:40 Bryan Carter drum solo

43:51 Kurt Introduces an exquisite Duke Ellington Composition- lyrics inspired by the profundity of Chicago tenor saxophonist, Von Freeman, & 13th century poet, Rumi  📜🕯️🍸
45:58 I Like The Sunrise (Duke Ellington; orig. lyrics by Kurt Elling) 🌄
“The singing is a drop…in oceans of seas
— Grace keeps it moving, through bodies like these…” // 🌊 🎼 The music in every sunrise, makes a space inside the skies. . for setting free. 🗝️ 🙏🏾 🙌🏼

*~*~*~*~*~*~*~*~*~*~*~
53:23 September In the Rain 🍂🌂 (Harry Warren; lyr. Al Dubin) / 54:40 Jacob Chung tenor sax solo / 56:43 Emmet Cohen piano solo 
58:53  Elling Keeps the Beat & Quintessence of the ‘Beat Generation’ 🗣️🎼🫰🏽— Jack Kerouac’s ‘On the Road’ 🌬️🌃 - “The Arrival to Great Chicago, Wee-Hour Escapades at Anita O’Day’s, The Sonic Engineering of George Shearing” (getting hip to part 3 chapter 10 of Kerouac’s work) / 1:02:00 Cats Return to the Bridge  🌉
1:05:00 Appreciation to Sponsors for Evening Performance
 
1:07:21 In The Still Of The Night (Cole Porter) - ft. Jacob Chung, tenor saxophone / 1:11:31 EC piano solo / 🔥 1:14:22 Trading with Jacob & Bryan / 1:15:20 Brian Carter drum solo / 1:16:40 Jacob brings it home

1:18:35 Kurt Reflects on the Genius of the legendary “Weather Report” 👑 // The blessing of touring & the permission to pen lyrics to the group’s compositions & solos // 🦉💡 A great lyric is birthed from attention to the ‘local headlines’ in one’s own experience …which, in turn, bears a larger universality 🌍

1:23:57 Current Affairs (Joe Zawinul; orig. lyrics by Kurt Elling) 🗞️ - piano introduction, Emmet Cohen / 1:26:22 Kurt embarks on vocals— “the curtain falls & the journey calls” / 1:29:28 Emmet Cohen piano solo ( - an orchestral offering 🎹🙏🏼💎) / 1:32:26 Kurt comes to a final crescendo 

1:34:44  In Walked Bud (Thelonious Monk) - ft. the scat ferocity of Kurt Elling  // 1:37:15 Jacob Chung tenor sax solo // 1:38:28 Emmet Cohen piano solo // 1:40:16 some stride sorcery 🎹 //1:41:34  Serious trading btwn. JC, BC, EC, and KE 🔥🎶 // 1:43:12 A magnetic Norris bass solo 🏆 

1:44:24 Kurt Takes Us Home in Emmet’s Climate controlled Sanctuary of Swing — Making the Scene & Keeping it Cleean….spreading those vibes ever-so brightly and politely✌🏽♥️🌍
Pouring out Emmet’s Place Love for Kurt’s forthcoming Broadway appearance in Hadestown
1:48:12 A Safe, Swinging Send-Off with the Cats

jlM0jR4OGnk.title: Emmet Cohen WBGO Spotlight!
jlM0jR4OGnk.description: Tonight, Morning Jazz host Gary Walker kicks off the WBGO March Fund Drive live with pianist Emmet Cohen, bassist Russell Hall, and special guest, saxophonist Melissa Aldana, live from Emmet’s home in Harlem! You can support the public radio station at WBGO.org/support or by calling 1-800-499-9246. This drive, WBGO is partnering with the Jazz Foundation of America to support their COVID-19 Musicians' Emergency Fund.

Live at Emmet’s Place vol. 12 - ft. Veronica Swift (‘Vintage Quarantine Swing’ — an Emmet’s Place gem) 💎🏆✨

0:00 Introduction of the Trio (Emmet Cohen, piano; Russell Hall, bass; Kyle Poole, drums)
1:13 Time On My Hands (Vincent Youmans; lyr. Harold Adamson, Mack Gordon; Ahmad Jamal) 
7:50 Be-Bop (Dizzy Gillespie) 🎶
15:35 Emmet Cohen Welcomes the Live-Stream Viewers
16:33 Veronica Swift Joins the Living Room Stage (in her Emmet’s Place debut)
17:45 A Little Taste (Johnny Hodges; lyr. Dave Frishberg) 🎶
23:25 The Man I Love (George Gershwin) 🎶
29:20 Veronica reflects on quarantine life 2020 & revisiting love of film writing
30:35 Lover, Come Back to Me (Sigmund Romberg; lyr. Oscar Hammerstein)  - ft. Russell Hall, bass 
36:37 Veronica talks about the impact of challenging times // the Trio supports justice in the spirit of the Black American musical tradition 
38:43 Veronica Introduces Mel Tormé’s, “A Stranger in Town”
39:32 A Stranger in Town (Mel Tormé) 🎶
46:59 I Don’t Want to Cry Anymore (Victor Shertzinger; lyr. Billie Holiday) 🎶
52:33 Emmet Welcomes Benny Benack, III, to the living room 
53:27 I’m Getting Sentimental Over You (Ned Washington; lyr. George Bassman; Tommy Dorsey & Orchestra) -ft. Benny Benack, trumpet 🎶
1:05:25 Veronica & Benny share their love of vocalese & the masters of the art, with “Social Call”
1:06:30 Social Call (comp. Gigi Gryce; vocalese lyrics Benny Benack, III; Jon Hendricks) 🎶
1:13:55 No, Not Much (Robert Allen; lyr. Al Stillman)  - ft. Veronica Swift, duo w/ Russel Hall, bass 🎶
1:19:05 Kyle Poole shares words in tribute to ‘friend & force’ at Small’s jazz club, Sabu Price
1:21:11 ‘Round Midnight (Thelonious Monk) 🎶
1:30:33 Emmet highlights Veronica’s versatility // Gratitude for support // Encouragement of all listeners to pursue justice in these difficult times 
1:33:50 Guess Who’s In Town (Ethel Waters, James P. Johnson / comp. J.C. Johnson, Andy Razaf) 🎶
1:38:03 Blues send-off // Things Ain’t What They Used to Be (Mercer Ellington; lyr. Ted Persons) - ft. all musicians 🎶

*~*~*~*~*~*~*~*~*~*~*~*~*~*~*~*~*~*~

PbVjqqw10mE.title: Remembering Funmi Ononaiye ❤️
PbVjqqw10mE.description:
This week we lost a true giant— Funmi Ononaiye. He had seemingly endless layers and knowledge, and was omnipresent in the many corners of New York’s vast music scene. A master DJ, percussionist, former A&R executive, chief programmer for Jazz at Lincoln Center, a fixture on the national jazz scene, and forever dedicated to seeking and spreading positive vibrations, Funmi left his mark on so many.

Rarely do you come across a human being with such a forcefield of optimism and hope surrounding them. Funmi lived his life connecting people— artists, musicians, thinkers, healers, community leaders, and anyone else who wanted to participate in his world. 

We became friends in my early days in New York City in 2012. I’ve never seen someone appear at more gigs than Funmi, always there support and encourage musicians. The music truly fueled his soul. If you wanted to know if a band was hitting a groove, all you had to do was watch the way Funmi moved.

Funmi cherished time spent documenting in the recording studio. He loved to be part of the studio magic— he once spontaneously showed up and played impromptu percussion on a Herlin Riley record. In fact, he came to so many record dates I was on. Often times the group would hit a rut, feel stuck, or face some sort of a musical/vibrational challenge. Funmi would arrive, and instantly clear the air with an insightful comment and a deep laugh. The musical path forward would always seem obvious in his presence. As abstract as it sounds, musicians just played better when he was in the room.

When the pandemic hit, we instantly became family. Underground music, culture, and camaraderie were so important to him, and he became an integral part of our “Live From Emmet’s Place” livestream. In the early days, it would just be Russell, Kyle, Funmi and me in the apartment. The trio would play to a physical audience of one (just Funmi), but he had a way of channeling the energy of those watching online, and make us feel like we were playing to a thousand energetic fans. 

He was so invested in what we were doing, and had an acute sense for just how impactful our stream would be for jazz globally. His early advice: keep it natural, funky, diverse, and full of love. Our text message thread became a constant brainstorm for discussing future livestream guests, concepts, how to pair different musicians, and other ways to improve upon what we had started. He was a huge force behind the scenes, and I’m eternally grateful for his brilliant guidance. I am certain he was a key factor in our success.

After his pancreatic cancer diagnosis, it was a painful and tragic decline, and extremely difficult for those who were closest to him. Even in sickness, he would connect his friends and family, visit jazz clubs, and look for the joys in life. I got to know his beautiful mother, his siblings and many other people in his orbit. Funmi exhibited extreme strength and will power up until the last breath. I never heard him complain once, and no matter how bad things got, each time I would ask him how he was doing, he’d reply, “give thanks.”

We will honor Funmi by remembering to give thanks and practice gratitude for all of the blessings in life, big or small. He taught us about the possibilities unlocked from unwavering positivity, and made us marvel at his boundless energy. He never owned a smartphone and was always fully engaged in the present moment. He would make daily delicious sea moss smoothies and share them with anybody he’d come across in his day. He’d pull out the Kashaka, his favorite percussion instrument from West Africa, and in minutes have everyone grooving and dancing in a circle around him.
 
Funmi made one final trek up the five flights to the Emmet’s Place on November 20, 2023 to see Herlin Riley, and join in on our Harlem Rent Party festivities. He introduced the show with a passionate speech, and left us with a final thought: “Make sure you get up out of your seat and do the DANCE.” Funmi’s dance lights an eternal fire under all who were blessed to know him. 

Rest in power Funmi Ononaiye.

14:21 The More I See You (Henry Warren; lyr. Mack Gordon) - ft. Bria Skonberg, vocals & trumpet  🤍🔭
25:32 Emmet Cohen Introduces the Musicians: Joe Farnsworth, drums; Philip Norris, bass; Chris Lewis, tenor saxophone; and Bria Skonberg, trumpet 
28:23 Bria Shares Her Warmth from the Living Room Stage // Her reflection of performances with Chris Lewis, uplifting children at the Louis Armstrong House  — reminding us that happiness is a choice we make 🙌🏽 🎯
29:17 Bria’s Introduces her blazing arrangement of a classic tune 🔥
30:00 Comes Love (Nothing Can Be Done) ♥️ (Sam Stept; lyr. Lew Brown & Charles Tobias) - ft. Bria Skonberg, trumpet; Chris Lewis, tenor sax; & Joe Farnsworth, drum solo
43:15 Recognition of Evening Sponsors
45:12 Body and Soul (Johnny Green; lyr. E. Heyman, R. Sour, F. Eaton) - ft. Chris Lewis, tenor saxophone, & his delivery of a gorgeous introduction 🎷🌃
1:00:48 You Are So Beautiful 💎 (Billy Preston, Bruce Fisher) - ft. Bria Skonberg, vocals & trumpet
1:09:01 Joe Farnsworth Honors Memory of Beloved Friend, Musician, & Master Trumpeter, James Rotondi 🤍// Recollection of Rotondi’s Impact During College Years // The excitement generated by Donald Byrd & Philly Joe Jones’ recording of “Cute” // Dedication of this precious tune to Brother Jim Rotondi’s memory 🎺
1:10:41 Cute (Neal Hefti, Stanley Styne)

1:33:54 Short Story (Kenny Dorham) - ft. Phil Norris, bass; Chris Lewis, tenor 
1:42:42 Night Time Is the Right Time (Napoleon ‘Nappy’ Brown; recrd. Ray Charles, 1958) // 🔹 Blues send-off - ft. Bria Skonberg, vocals & trumpet; Chris Lewis, tenor;  & all musicians 🔥🌌

23:10 Paquito highlights the role of Brazilian music in the ‘jazz’ tradition.

38:04 Paquito D’Rivera Recalls first encounter w/ Claudio Roditi, Brazilian valve trombonist // Diego Urcola shares a personal history with the valve trombone

56:46 Birks’ Works  (Dizzy Gillespie) - ft. P. D’Rivera, clarinet; D. Urcola, trumpet

1:11:37 Alfonsina y El Mar  (comp. Ariel Ramirez) - ft. Diego Urcola, trumpet 
1:22:14 Paquito D’Rivera shares the humorous unpredictability in the life of a musician. 
1:23:44 Alexander’s Ragtime Band, with intro (Irving Berlin) - ft. Paquito D’Rivera, clarinet & Diego Urcola, trumpet 
1:31:56 Paquito discusses tour with Diego & pianist Chucho Valdés, introducing his composition dedicated to Chucho “I Miss You Too”
1:33:33 I Miss You Too (comp. Paquito D’Rivera) 
1:44:32 Paquito Recognizes Mario Bauzá as one of Harlem’s most influential figures // his impact on Dizzy Gillespie & bebop movement & role as musical director of the Machito orchestra
1:45:46 Mambo Inn (comp. Mario Bauzá, Grace Sampson, & Bobby Woodlen)

LAVvhMwlVa4.title: Emmet a Cohen Trio Covid Era European Tour!
LAVvhMwlVa4.description:
In October of 2020, the Emmet Cohen Trio toured Europe. This was the first tour after the Covid lock-down by an American Artist. The tour was booked and facilitated by Music Works International 
. 

Emmet Cohen piano
Yasushi Nakamura double bass
Kyle Poole drums

Featuring appearances by Luigi Sidero, Piero Odorici, great food, good times, and grappa! 

And special tags of thanks to all the clubs who had us in to play on this amazing tour Cantina Bentivoglio Jazz Club Perugia Porgy & Bess Auditorium Mole Vanvitelliana - Ancona Blue Note Milano Mantova/italia Backnanger Bürgerhaus City of Basel Music Academy the birds eye jazz club Einstein St.Gallen King Georg Jazzclub Unterfahrt | Luigi Sidero Piero Odorici Emmet Cohen

0jeBIG2yDw4.title: Remembering Benny Golson (1929-2024)
0jeBIG2yDw4.description:
I first met Benny Golson when he visited the University of Miami in 2011. What initially struck me was how he carried himself, composed and elegant, his movement and speech draped in that off-the-cuff jazz hipness. He left a big impression on me. A direct beam to the founding fathers of modern jazz. Benny contained a wealth of stories, and recounted them with ease, like the time he and John Coltrane played “On the Sunny Side of the Street” for hours in his mother’s basement, only to have his mother peep in with a wish for Benny to be able to play more like John. My young, eager mind was inextricably captivated by personal jazz history anecdotes, finding them far more digestible than the assigned jazz history readings!

I knew I had to meet and find Benny Golson again, and as I quickly learned, one of best tools for becoming closer to any jazz master is to ask them to record. In the studio, money equals respect, and a mutual respect can be earned if the master digs your playing. I organized a record date for October of 2017, and nervously called Mr. Golson, humbly asking for his participation, and excited to let him know that his old friend Tootie Heath would be playing drums. A Philly connection.

“Oh, Tootie! He used to run around the backyard in those short-shorts!” Benny proclaimed. I laughed, but Benny’s tone shifted downward: “Thank you, but I’m afraid I can’t do the session. It would be far too expensive.” When I ask just how expensive it might be, Benny told me firmly that he would be $1000 per track. I took a mental snapshot of my bank account, did some quick calculations in my head, and with all the faux-poise in the world, told him that it would be my honor to have him on five tracks. He immediately and cheerfully agreed. I realized later that he had a unique style of negotiating— experienced in handling that sort of business, probably a big factor in his fruitful career. 

Bassist Russell Hall and I had goosebumps hearing Benny warm up on the recording day. Within just a few notes his sound was instantly distinguishable, colored with the influences of Coleman Hawkins and Don Byas, but recognizably his own. “Full, round, and mellow,” as he would describe it. He was a true improviser and a genius composer, who wouldn’t be found within miles of an overdone lick or cliché. The session was a success in my eyes (and ears), and Mr. Golson’s wistful and soul-stirring interpretation of “Embraceable You” was a particular highlight.

After the session, we exchanged a series of emails. Benny was the e-mail KING, a true wordsmith and intellectual. The consummate gentleman, he mentioned what a joy it was to meet my parents, said he would be calling me in the future, and left me with this kernel of advice: “Do continue the great work and effort as you unremittingly keep adding to the ineluctable success of your FORWARD MOTION. You have much to 'say.’”

Call me he did, and few months later Benny personally reached out and invited me to join his band for a run at the Jazz Standard. He handed me the book at soundcheck, but I already knew the tunes— Stablemates, Killer Joe, I Remember Clifford, Whisper Not, Along Came Betty, among others. He told intricate and detailed stories, entertained the audiences masterfully, and infused the banter with light hearted humor, sometimes picking up the mic and exclaiming, “well isn’t our pianist a show-off— there’s always one in the band!” 

The last time we played together was right before the pandemic, a mini-midwest run in January 2020. We had amazing concerts in Grand Rapids, MI and Cincinnati, OH, but a huge snowstorm befell the area prior to our gig in Iowa. Our flights had been canceled, and the only way to make it would have been to rent a car and drive eight hours in less than desirable conditions. He was one week shy of his 91st birthday, so I carefully asked him what he’d like to do. I remember distinctly, “Emmet, this is your band, and I’m 100% with you guys. If we decide to drive, it’s totally alright with me.”

We ultimately decided to cancel and fly home when things calmed down. In retrospect, driving shouldn’t have even been a viable option, but it speaks to Benny Golson’s strength, courage and belief in the music. For the old guard, a commitment was something to be honored and upheld, respect and reputation were the most valuable currencies. He lived his life by those values. A a once-in-a-lifetime human being, he filled so many hearts with love over his 95 years on earth, and left us a body of work to play, listen to and remember him by. I Remember Benny— with love, respect and eternal grace.

a_zvBpmhzzg.title: Emmet’s Place Education: Grant Stewart
a_zvBpmhzzg.description:
This month, we are pleased to feature a true master musician, Grant Stewart.  He has performed internationally with Jimmy Cobb, Harold Mabern, Louis Hayes, Curtis Fuller, Renee Fleming, Clark Terry, Bob Mover, Etta Jones, Bill Charlap, Lewis Nash, Peter Washington, Brad Mehldau, Russell Malone, Larry Goldings, Peter Bernstein, Harry Connick, Mickey Roker, Jimmy Lovelace, Cecil Payne, Dick Hymen, Herb Geller and was a member of the last Al Grey Sextet.  For more: https://grantstewartjazz.com/about/

Check out Grant's new school, the Tribeca Jazz Institute: https://www.churchstreetschool.org/jazz

Thank you to our sponsor, DJ DeProspero!

Hosted by Bryan Eng.

dloNN1yZX0s.title: Legendary Pianist Barry Harris Plays "My Heart Stood Still"
dloNN1yZX0s.description:
October 21 & 22 the trio will be playing at The Appel Room at Jazz at Lincoln Center with special guests Johnny O’Neal, Mary Stallings and Lucy Yeghiazaryan! Four years ago we played the same room with Russell Hall’s Monk tribute, and we were joined by one of the greatest pianists of all time, Barry Harris. Here is a clip from our rehearsal with him!

Hope to see some friends in October.. Tickets can be found using at https://2022.jazz.org/emmet-cohen-trio-and-friends

1:08:49 La Vie en Rose 🌹(Edith Piaf) - ft. vocals & drums, Herlin Riley

0:00 Hangin’ in and Jivin’ (Nicholas Payton)
9:17 Tea For Two (Vincent Youmans; lyr. Irving Caesar) 🫖🔥
20:11 Emmet Cohen Introduces the Musicians: Nicholas Payton, trumpet; Joe Dyson, drums; and Philip Norris, bass
22:00 Levin’s Lope (Nicholas Payton) — a composition written for beloved bassist, Sir Ron Levin Carter  🎻
28:03 Pinocchio (Wayne Shorter) — (ft. audio: Wayne Shorter reflects on the conversations & chemistry shared w/ Miles Davis & Herbie Hancock — Davis’ instructions to Shorter, inspiring future recordings: “Bring the book.”) 📓🎶
36:05 Introduction of Nicholas Payton’s “Notes From the Zen Gangster” - Payton’s insights & hard-earned wisdom acquired as a musician, activist, & mouthpiece of Black American music: “A mistake is the truth trying to come out” 🖤🔥🕉
37:40 Big George (Nicholas Payton) — a composition written for iconic tenor saxophonist, George Coleman 🎷
47:23 Q for Quincy Jones (Nicholas Payton) — a composition written for the legendary composer & producer // 51:02 🎹 Nicholas Payton joins Emmet Cohen at the keys
54:01 Gratitude for the heartfelt sponsorship of this evening’s performance by the faithful community of Monday night music-lovers (TGIM)
55:07 Feed the Fire 🔥 (Geri Allen) — (ft. audio: composer & pianist, Geri Allen describes the musical landscape of Detroit - the fluid movement between genres by members of Motown & diverse communities of musicians // the openness to embrace new disciplines & settings) 🌍 🔊
1:03:33 You Go To My Head (J. Fred Coots; lyr. Haven Gillespie) 🥂
1:13:25 Oleo (Sonny Rollins)
1:22:47 Bleek’s Blues (Nicholas Payton)
1:33:20 Struttin’ With Some Barbecue (comp. Lillian Hardin Armstrong; lyr. Don Raye) ⚜️❤️‍🔥
1:39:20 Body & Soul  (Johnny Green; lyr. E. Heyman, Robert Sour, F. Eaton)
1:47:38 Emmet Expresses Gratitude to Nicholas Payton for his inspiration of the music scene as a mentor & master; as one invested in the organization of thought, efficiency of notes, & the dedication to choices. 🎯🔥
1:49:22 Blues send-off / S.K.J. (Milt Jackson) - (in tribute to our dearly missed Funmi Ononaiye 🤍🌎♾️)

Err1S4ETPh4.title: Live From Emmet's Place Vol. 87 - Ukraine Benefit (Jazz4Ukraine)
Err1S4ETPh4.description:
Donate to Jazz4Ukraine here: https://secure.givelively.org/donate/miami-jazz-cooperative-inc/jazz4ukraine

Within two days, 100% of donations received are remitted to World Central Kitchen, the renowned international nonprofit organization founded by Chef José Andrés.  WCK is feeding displaced Ukrainians in Ukraine, Poland, Hungary, and neighboring countries.  Donations are matched by the TripAdvisor Foundation.  Text Jazz4Ukraine to 44321 or visit the link above to donate!  Email questions to jazz4ukraine@miamijazz.org

cjojfOEtaeg.title: Emmet Cohen Masters Legacy Series Vol. 4: George Coleman
cjojfOEtaeg.description:
This is a mini documentary featuring music and interviews from Emmet Cohen's signature project, Masters Legacy Series. Volume 4 features legendary saxophonist, George Coleman! The Masters Legacy Series celebrates legendary jazz artists from an era long gone by documenting, featuring, and preserving their artistry and excellence in the later stages of their careers.

This record features:
Emmet Cohen (piano)
George Coleman (tenor sax)
Russell Hall (acoustic bass)
Bryan Carter (drums)

Track info:

1. Trinkle Tinkle (9:09) – Thelonious Monk
2. I Surrender Dear (7:26)- Harry Barris 
3. For Big G (8:06)- Emmet Cohen, George Coleman
4. Don't Blame Me (9:35)- Jimmy McHugh
5. On Green Dolphin Street (9:52)- Bronislaw Kaper
6. Ugly Beauty (8:56)- Thelonious Monk 
7. Triste (5:28)- Antonio Carlos Jobim
8. You've Changed (6:13)- Carl Fischer
9. Db Blues (9:54)

Visit EmmetCohen.com/shop to order physical or mp3 versions!

Release date: December 2019

lJORMrMZds0.title: Emmet Cohen Masters Legacy Series Vol. 3: Benny Golson & Tootie Heath
lJORMrMZds0.description:
Pianist Emmet Cohen's signature project, Masters Legacy Series continues with Volume 3. Benny Golson and Tootie Heath are both featured on this release. The Masters Legacy Series celebrates the legendary jazz artists from an era long gone by documenting, featuring, and preserving their artistry and excellence in the later stages of their careers.

Here, we sit down with Albert Tootie Heath for a conversation and an inside look at the making of this record. Release date: December 2019

Emmet Cohen: Piano
Benny Golson: Tenor Saxophone
Albert “Tootie” Heath: Drums
Russell Hall: Acoustic Bass
Corcoran Holt: Acoustic Bass (Tracks 8 & 12)

1. It Don't Mean A Thing (If It Ain't Got That Swing) (5:51)
2. Lady Bird (5:45)- Tadd Dameron
3. Promise Of The Sun (4:51) Herbie Hancock 
4.    Reminiscing I (1:42)
5. Woody 'N You (4:20)- Dizzy Gillespie 
6. Embraceable You (8:44)- George Gershwin
7.    Reminiscing II (1:39)
8. Straight Street (6:12)- John Coltrane
9. For Seven's Sake (4:22)- Albert “Tootie” Heath
10. Just You, Just Me (7:40)- Jesse Greer
11.   Reminiscing III (1:05)
12. Charleston (3:50)- James P. Johnson

i20sujN_JWs.title: Emmet Cohen Russell Hall Evan Sherman give a master class in Cleveland!
i20sujN_JWs.description: It is always beautiful to affect different types of audiences in various parts of the country, and even more important to connect with students during the daytime while on tour. We often give masterclasses/performance lectures to both college and high school students. In the video above, we asked the class for three emotions to help inform how we play Thelonious Monk’s Round Midnight. The students gave us “anger, nostalgia, and hope” in that order. Can you feel it??
